article.title: IELTS Writing Task 2 essay structure
article.description: A high-scoring Task 2 essay typically has 4 paragraphs: (1) Introduction — paraphrase the question and state your position, (2) Body paragraph 1 — first main idea with an example, (3) Body paragraph 2 — second main idea with an example, (4) Conclusion — summarize your position. Aim for 250+ words. Common mistake: writing a conclusion that introduces a brand-new idea instead of summarizing.
article.content: A high-scoring Task 2 essay typically has 4 paragraphs: (1) Introduction — paraphrase the question and state your position, (2) Body paragraph 1 — first main idea with an example, (3) Body paragraph 2 — second main idea with an example, (4) Conclusion — summarize your position. Aim for 250+ words. Common mistake: writing a conclusion that introduces a brand-new idea instead of summarizing.

generated.caption:
Facing IELTS Task 2? A strong essay structure makes all the difference! ✨

Aim for 4 paragraphs: an Intro (paraphrase & state your position), two Body paragraphs (main ideas + examples), and a Conclusion (summarize your position). A common mistake? Don't introduce *new* ideas in your conclusion! Target 250+ words.

Save this tip for your next essay! Follow @EnglishVault for daily lessons! 📚
generated.script: Are you making this common mistake in your IELTS Writing Task 2 essays, introducing a new idea in your conclusion, when you should be summarizing your position instead. A high-scoring essay typically has four paragraphs, starting with an introduction where you paraphrase the question and state your position. You then have two body paragraphs, each presenting a main idea with an example, such as, the implementation of renewable energy sources has led to a significant reduction in carbon emissions in many countries. To ace your Task 2 essay, remember to summarize your position in the conclusion, and follow us for more tips to improve your IELTS writing skills.

slides.text: Are you structuring your IELTS essays correctly?

slides.heading: Introduction to IELTS Writing Task 2 essay structure
slides.text: A high-scoring Task 2 essay typically has 4 paragraphs.
slides.hindiMeaning: एक उच्च स्कोर वाले टास्क 2 निबंध में आमतौर पर 4 अनुच्छेद होते हैं

slides.heading: Breakdown of the essay structure
slides.text: The 4 paragraphs are: Introduction, Body paragraph 1, Body paragraph 2, and Conclusion. Aim for 250+ words.

slides.wrong: The conclusion of my essay will discuss a new idea about the topic.
slides.right: In conclusion, my essay has shown that this topic is important.

slides.heading: Conclusion summary
slides.text: A common mistake is writing a conclusion that introduces a brand-new idea instead of summarizing. Make sure to summarize your position in the conclusion.
Now that you know the IELTS Writing Task 2 essay structure, start practicing and improve your score!
